title: QR Scanning for column level

posts.author: Sana (Sanjana Prathapa)
posts.cookedHtml:
Im using enterprise version now. My client wants to have this feature. Can i know if this is possible.

  • We have boards, inside them several columns.
  • I want to scan a specific QR related to a column and once scanned it will capture the time and date as of scanned.

Thank You

posts.author: ZakiImtiaz (Zaki | readylogic.co)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello @Sana ,
I believe it may not be possible to achieve this functionality solely using Monday.com. You might need a third-party tool to capture the date and time during the scan. This will be feasible only if you have a solid understanding of APIs, as your question indicates a requirement for very specific data.

posts.author: workflowboost (Bartosz @ Workflow Boost)
posts.cookedHtml:
We recently released an app called QR Flow that allows you to do that. Here is step-by-step how you can do it using this app:

  1. Prepare a files column to save QR codes and a date column to save the scan time. Here is a board I’ll be using:

image1034×304 44.6 KB

  1. Create automation for creating QR codes. You can name QR code whatever you like and select single-use or multi-use based on your use case (single scan QR codes can be used only once)

image1396×405 64.6 KB

  1. Create a custom automation using trigger introduced by the QR Flow app. As an action set “set date” action (monday.com native action). The name must much the name from the previous automation.

image794×414 50.5 KB

  1. Create a new item and see the QR code being generated (first automation will run):

image1061×317 53.3 KB

image1647×667 88.5 KB

  1. Scan the generated QR code and see the “Scan time” column being updated (second automation will run):

image946×304 55.5 KB

image1684×431 47.8 KB

In case of any issues or questions, I’d be happy to help.

posts.author: Avo_FluidApps (Avo)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @Sana! Just came across this thread, I know it’s a bit old, but I wanted to share a solution that might help in case you’re still looking or others find this useful.

I have the exact solution for this using QR Item Tracker on monday.com - it automatically record scan time when a QR Code is scanned.

It aligns perfectly with what you described.

Feel free to reach out or you can explore it risk-free with our 14-day free trial.
